discussion_comments.comment_body: What do you think of having `report_shutdown` take a `ReportShutdownRequest` as its argument, so this case is unreachable?

That would make this neater, although I don't see this being used for other APIs. I wonder if the right thing to do here is to just expose two distinct methods: `report_clean_shutdown` and `report_errored_shutdown` that handle this.
Not sure I like this more but at least we through earlier if you call the command with the wrong arguments now. 🤷🏽‍♀️

discussion_comments.comment_body:
not blocking

1) this should take owned strings, not `str`s
2) It should probably be a struct with named fields (very easy to confused `user_id` and `user_email` as currently writte

I'm fairly certain that we implicitly rely on this event being emitted once per block - as that's been the case since the event was introduced years ago.

It's probably safer to introduce a new, more tightly scoped event variant and just ensure that it's probably broadcasted to dependent logic that is currently solely relying on this event to subscribe to updated working directory.

Good call — pushed 2eaf7eb that does exactly that.

OSC 7 now emits a new `BlockWorkingDirectoryUpdated` event variant instead of reusing `BlockMetadataReceived`. The new event mirrors the `BlockMetadataReceivedEvent` payload (block_metadata, block_index, is_after_in_band_command, is_done_bootstrapping) so CWD-dependent listeners can reuse the same handling, but it is opt-in:

- `TerminalView` (tab title / repo detection / git status / prompt refresh)
- `ActiveSession` (UpdatedPwd emission)
- `BlocklistAIContextModel` (directory context)

…each subscribe to **both** events via a small shared helper, so OSC 7 still drives every listener that genuinely cares about the CWD.

Subscribers tied to precmd semantics — most importantly the requested-command finish detector in `ai/blocklist/action_model/execute/shell_command.rs` — are unchanged and continue listening only to `BlockMetadataReceived`, preserving the once-per-block contract you flagged.

The block test was updated to assert that `set_current_working_directory` emits `BlockWorkingDirectoryUpdated` **and** that it does **not** emit `BlockMetadataReceived`, so any future regression on this contract will fail loudly.
Are we currently (de)serializing this anywhere? If so, this rename may break existing data

We actually don't serialize this anywhere else; the only other place that `UserQueryMode`'s sent over the wire is via the multi-agent API, but this is proto-based and the query mode is converted directly into a proto type. I do agree that we should be more explicit here though to guard unexpected behavior in the future. 

Recent commit should remove this and add a custom serializer for the public API
Do we actually need an inner here? We could just have the DiffStateModel itself be an enum right?
Hmm fair - the initial thinking was to keep the local and remote model handles themselves private and encourage only the pub apis but I don't think it's that big of deal
